description: Wedding: save-the-date has basic info, formal invitation adds full venue details and schedule

expected.title: Sarah & Michael's Wedding
expected.location: Seaside Resort - Garden Pavilion, 123 Ocean Drive

actual.title: Sarah & Michael's Wedding
actual.location: Seaside Resort - Garden Pavilion, 123 Ocean Drive
actual.description: Save the date! Wedding celebration for Sarah and Michael Ceremony at 4pm, reception follows at 5:30pm. Cocktail attire. RSVP by June 15. Hotel block available under 'Chen-Anderson wedding'.

content_parts.text:
You are merging calendar event data from multiple emails.

**Existing Event:**
- Title: Sarah & Michael's Wedding
- Start: 2026-07-25T00:00:00
- End: 2026-07-25T23:59:59
- All Day: True
- Location: Seaside Resort
- Description: Save the date! Wedding celebration for Sarah and Michael
- Importance: action_required

**New Information (source type: update):**
- Title: Sarah & Michael's Wedding
- Start: 2026-07-25T16:00:00
- End: 2026-07-25T22:00:00
- All Day: False
- Location: Seaside Resort - Garden Pavilion, 123 Ocean Drive
- Description: Ceremony at 4pm, reception follows at 5:30pm. Cocktail attire. RSVP by June 15. Hotel block available under 'Chen-Anderson wedding'.
- Importance: action_required

**Merge Rules:**
1. If source_type is "cancellation", prefix title with "CANCELLED: " (if not already)
2. Prefer specific time (e.g., 6-7pm) over all-day
3. Combine descriptions, keeping all relevant info (append new info)
4. Use most specific location (longer address usually better)
5. Keep newer times if they differ (updates)
6. For importance: keep the highest level (action_required > fyi)

Output JSON with merged event data:
{
    "title": "...",
    "start_datetime": "...",
    "end_datetime": "...",
    "all_day": true/false,
    "location": "...",
    "description": "...",
    "importance": "action_required" or "fyi"
}

    "importance": "action_required"
}
